event.title: Replace with event title
event.context: 2-5 paragraphs combining overview and structural background with inline citations such as {{cite|example2024|pp. 10-20|opening-scene}}. Store tooltip excerpts under references[].excerpts. Who the actors are, why this dilemma arose, and what conditions shaped it.
event.triggering_event: The specific proximate event that forced the strategic choice. Narrow and concrete.

timeline.description: Example precondition event with inline citation {{cite|example2024|p. 5|founding-order}}.
Example of a multi-month crisis window; use end_date for ranges.

core_dilemma.summary: Frame the central strategic dilemma in one sentence.

actors.name: First actor
actors.description: Describe the actor's position, motivations, and structural role.

strategies.mechanism: How was it supposed to work?

outcomes.description: Overall narrative of what happened and why.

assessment.verdict:
**Opening summary.** Bottom-line judgement: which strategy was superior, why, and with what caveats.

**Second paragraph summary.** Each paragraph begins with a bold summary header, like the event.context field.

historiography.interpretation: A scholarly reading with inline citations.

cited_authors.name: Surname, Name
cited_authors.bio: Short bibliographical or biographical introduction for this cited author.

defined_terms.definition: One-to-three sentence definition of a proper noun, operation, slogan, or technical label vital to the record but not explained in the prose. The UI auto-links every exact-case occurrence of the term in text fields to this definition.
